subject: [PATCH RFC] sunrpc: hardcode pool_mode to pernode, remove other modes

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the distinction between 'auto' and 'default' pool modes is unclear, describing it as 'whacky'.

Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that setting a deprecated pool mode should not cause module loading to fail, as failing to load would be more painful than loading and having different thread affinity behavior.
reviews.raw_body:
OK, there seems to be a difference between "auto" and "default". That's
a little whacky.

---

I'm saying that setting a deprecated pool mode should not cause
module loading to fail at all. Failing to load will be more painful
than loading and having different thread affinity behavior.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch removes the SVC_POOL_* enum and mode selection heuristic, simplifying the pool map functions to always use the pernode path. The percpu mode is removed as it created excessive pools relative to the number of threads most deployments run. The module parameter and netlink interfaces are preserved for backward compatibility, but writing any value other than 'pernode' will return an error. The default pool mode is hardcoded to pernode, which provides one pool per node with proper thread affinity and NUMA-local memory allocation.
